volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 4.7.1124e - Section 4.7.1170)

sections.label: Section 4.7.1124e
sections.translation: Because that booty ’ s mine .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1125
sections.original: scórtum quaerit , hábet argentum . iam ádmordere hunc míhi lubet .
sections.translation: He ’ s in search of a mistress he has got money . I already long to fix my teeth in him .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1126
sections.translation: Are you going to devour him already ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1126b
sections.translation: While he ’ s fresh , while he ’ s in the habit of giving away , while he ’ s warm upon it , ’ tis proper for him to be gobbled up . The good men keep me poor , the bad ones support me ; the virtuous are a benefit to the public , the debauched to myself .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1130
sections.original: Malúm quod tibí di dabúnt , sic sceléstu ’ (s e) s .
sections.translation: A mischief may the Gods send you ; such a villain are you .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1131
sections.original: Me nunc commoror , quom has foris non ferio , ut sciam ,
sections.translation: I ’ m now delaying myself , in not knocking at this door , that I may know whether or no Ballio is at home now .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1132
sections.original: Vénus mi haec bona dat , quom hós huc adigit
sections.translation: Venus bestows upon me these blessings , when she drives hither these haters of money , these who quite long for losses , who carefully pamper themselves and their youthful age , eat , drink , and wench . Of quite different dispositions are they and you .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1136
sections.translation: ( Calls out , going up to the door .) Hallo ! where are you ? Hallo ! where are you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1136b
sections.original: Hic quidem ad me récta habet rectám viam .
sections.translation: Why , he ’ s going straight up towards my house .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1138
sections.original: bene ego ab hoc praedatus ibo ; novi , bona scaevast mihi .
sections.translation: I shall come off finely loaded with plunder from this fellow ; I know it ’ s a lucky omen for me .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1139
sections.translation: Does any one come to open this door ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1139b
sections.original: Heus chlamydate , quid istic debetur tibi ?
sections.translation: Hallo ! you in the scarf , what ’ s owing you at that house ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1140
sections.translation: I ’ m enquiring for Ballio , the procurer , the master of the house .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1141
sections.original: Quisquis es , adulescens , operam fac compendi quaerere .
sections.translation: Whoever you are , young man , make short work of your enquiries .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1142
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1142b
sections.original: Quia tute ipsus ipsum praesens praesentem vides .
sections.translation: Because he himself in person sees you in his presence before him .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1143
sections.translation: What , are you he ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1143b
sections.original: Chlamydate , cáve sis tibi a curvo infortunio
sections.translation: You in the scarf , take you care , please , of some crooked misfortune , and point your finger [*] at him ; this fellow is a procurer .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1144b
sections.translation: And this is an honest man .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1145
sections.original: sed , tu , bone vir , flagitare saepe clamore in foro ,
sections.translation: ( To SIMO .) But you , worthy fellow , are many a time being hunted after in the Forum with noise enough , when you haven ’ t a groat in the world , unless this procurer here comes to help you a bit .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1147
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you address yourself to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1147b
sections.translation: I do address you . What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1148
sections.translation: You to take this money .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1148b
sections.translation: Already have I extended my hand , if you are going to give it .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1149
sections.original: Accipe : hic sunt quinque argenti lectae numeratae minae .
sections.translation: Take it ; here are five picked minae of silver counted out .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1150
sections.original: hoc tibi erus me iussit ferre Polymachaeroplagides ,
sections.translation: ( Gives him the money .) This did my master , Polymachaeroplagides . order me to deliver to you , the sum which he was owing , and that you were to send Phœnicium with me .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1152
sections.translation: Your master ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1152b
sections.translation: I say to that effect .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1152c
sections.translation: The Captain ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1152d
sections.translation: I speak to that effect .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1152e
sections.translation: The Macedonian ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1153
sections.translation: Such is the fact , I say .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1153b
sections.translation: Polymachaeroplagides sent you to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1154
sections.translation: You say what ’ s fact .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1154b
sections.translation: To give me this money ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1154c
sections.translation: If you really are

sections.label: Section 4.7.1155
sections.translation: the procurer Ballio .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1155b
sections.original: Atque ut a me mulierem tu abduceres ?
sections.translation: And for you to take away the woman from me ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1156
sections.translation: Even so .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1156b
sections.translation: Did he say that it was Phœnicium ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1156c
sections.translation: You remember it exactly .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1156d
sections.translation: Wait there ; I ’ ll return to you this instant .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1157b
sections.original: At maturate propera , nam propero : vides
sections.translation: Make haste , then , with all speed , for I ’ m in a hurry . ’ Tis now late in the day , d ’ ye see ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1158b
sections.translation: I see ; still I wish to call this person aside . Do you only wait there ; I ’ ll return to you this instant . ( He takes SIMO on one side .) What ’ s to be done now , Simo ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1160
sections.original: quid agimus ? manufesto hunc hominem teneo , qui argentum attulit .
sections.translation: What are we to do ? I ’ ve detected this fellow that has brought the money in the fact .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1161
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1161b
sections.original: An nescis quae sit haec res ?
sections.translation: And don ’ t you understand what this plan is ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1161c
sections.translation: About as much as the most ignorant do .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1162
sections.original: Pseudolus tuos allegavit hunc , quasi a Macedonio
sections.translation: Your servant , Pseudolus , has sent this fellow on the message , as though he was from the Macedonian Captain .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1163b
sections.translation: Have you got the money from the fellow ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1163c
sections.translation: Do you ask about that which you see ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1164
sections.original: Heus , memento ergo dimidium istinc mihi de praeda dare :
sections.translation: Harkye , remember to give me one half of that plunder .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1165
sections.translation: It ’ s right that that should be in common .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1165b
sections.original: Quid , malum ? id totum tuom est .
sections.translation: Why , plague on ’ t , ’ tis all your own [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1166
sections.translation: How soon are you going to attend to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1166b
sections.original: Tibi do equidem . quid nunc mi es auctor , Simo ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll attend to you , indeed . ( Aside .) What now do you advise me to do , Simo ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1167
sections.translation: Let ’ s make some fun of this counterfeit spy , even until he himself shall be sensible that he is being made a fool of .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1169
sections.original: Sequere . quid ais ? nempe tu illíus servos es ?
sections.translation: Follow me . ( To HARPAX .) What have you to say ? You are his servant , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.1169b
sections.translation: Most certainly .

sections.label: Section 4.7.1170
sections.translation: At what price did he purchase you ?
